summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that an integer overflow occurs at line 95 when processing a maliciously crafted protobuf message, resulting in a panic and denial of service.
expected_security_impact: The PoC should demonstrate a panic triggered by integer overflow when processing a message with a crafted length field near usize::MAX. In debug mode, Rust panics on the overflow at line 95. In release mode, the overflow wraps around, passes the safety check, but panics on subsequent buffer operations.
actual_observed_result: The exploit successfully triggered a panic with message 'attempt to add with overflow' in debug mode at line 95, confirming the integer overflow vulnerability. In release mode with overflow-checks enabled, the same panic occurs. The vulnerability allows any network attacker to crash the application by sending a single malformed message.
verification_method: The verify.sh script builds and runs the exploit in both debug and release modes. The exploit uses std::panic::catch_unwind to capture and confirm the panic. Success is indicated by the panic message 'attempt to add with overflow' or a panic in the buffer split operation, demonstrating the vulnerability is exploitable.
evidence_produced_by_run: Panic triggered with message 'attempt to add with overflow' in debug mode
The panic occurs at the exact vulnerable line (line 95) where message_length + varint_length is computed
The attack requires only a single network message with crafted varint-encoded length
Both debug and release builds with overflow-checks are affected

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository (https://github.com/libp2p/rust-libp2p.git) on the host, checks out the pinned commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo. Alternatively, if /repo exists, it uses that mounted directory directly.
